title: GDPR DPIA Completion Tracking Against High-Risk Processing

compliance.assurance_rationale: GDPR Art.35(7) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.35(7) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.1.32: GDPR DPIA Completion Tracking Against High-Risk Processing.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.1.32' running on gdpr_ropa_register.csv, gdpr_dpia_register.csv, change records snow:change_request, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE high_risk="true"
| lookup gdpr_dpia_register.csv processing_activity OUTPUT dpia_status, dpia_completed_date
| eval dpi_gap=case(
    dpia_status="completed", "OK",
    dpia_status="in_progress", "IN_PROGRESS",
    1=1, "MISSING")
| join processing_activity type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-90d
    | where match(lower(short_description),"(?i)go.live|launch|migrate")
    | stats latest(start_date) as last_major_change by cmdb_ci
    | rename cmdb_ci as system_name
]
| where dpi_gap!="OK"
| table processing_activity, system_name, dpi_gap, dpia_status, last_major_change
description: Where processing is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall carry out a DPIA prior to processing. This use case reconciles the ROPA, system go-live dates, and DPIA register to flag processing that started or changed materially without a completed DPIA.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Keep `high_risk` flags on ROPA entries per Art. 35(3); (2) join DPIA register by processing activity; (3) correlate major changes with DPIA refresh policy; (4) alert DPO on `MISSING` before production changes; (5) align with UC-22.1.14 for consolidated DPIA coverage views. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:change_request`; Lookups: `gdpr_dpia_register`, `gdpr_dpia_register.csv`, `gdpr_ropa_register`, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`.
visualization: Table (missing DPIAs), Single value (high-risk gaps), Pie chart (status distribution), Timeline (go-live vs DPIA dates). Key fields: `dpi_gap`, `dpia_status`, `last_major_change`, `processing_activity`, `system_name`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.object, All_Changes.command | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: False positives are usually explained by an approved process change, training activity, or time-bound exception recorded in the GRC or change system.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`, `gdpr_dpia_register.csv`, change records `snow:change_request`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Keep `high_risk` flags on ROPA entries per Art. 35(3); (2) join DPIA register by processing activity; (3) correlate major changes with DPIA refresh policy; (4) alert DPO on `MISSING` before production changes; (5) align with UC-22.1.14 for consolidated DPIA coverage views.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE high_risk="true"
| lookup gdpr_dpia_register.csv processing_activity OUTPUT dpia_status, dpia_completed_date
| eval dpi_gap=case(
    dpia_status="completed", "OK",
    dpia_status="in_progress", "IN_PROGRESS",
    1=1, "MISSING")
| join processing_activity type=left [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-90d
    | where match(lower(short_description),"(?i)go.live|launch|migrate")
    | stats latest(start_date) as last_major_change by cmdb_ci
    | rename cmdb_ci as system_name
]
| where dpi_gap!="OK"
| table processing_activity, system_name, dpi_gap, dpia_status, last_major_change
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR DPIA Completion Tracking Against High-Risk Processing (Art. 35(7))** — Where processing is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall carry out a DPIA prior to processing. This use case reconciles the ROPA, system go-live dates, and DPIA register to flag processing that started or changed materially without a completed DPIA.

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`, `gdpr_dpia_register.csv`, change records `snow:change_request`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dpi_gap** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where dpi_gap!="OK"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: dpi_gap, dpia_status, last_major_change, processing_activity, system_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing DPIAs), Single value (high-risk gaps), Pie chart (status distribution), Timeline (go-live vs DPIA dates).
grandmaExplanation: We where processing is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall carry out a DPIA prior to processing so we can show we meet our privacy duties when people ask for proof.
